summary: Split the tail of gm_1A36.c into new game-mode translation units gm_1BF9.c and gm_1BFA.c, marked gm_1BF9.c as Matching, and moved shared scene structs from gm_1A36.static.h into gm/types.h so the new file could use typed MajorScene/MinorScene data. The matched gm_1BF9 slice defines minor scene tables and implements gm_801BF948, gm_801BF9A8, and gm_801BFA3C with typed MinorScene accessors instead of raw M2C_FIELD offsets.
what_changed: Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so gm_1A36.c ends at 0x801BF948, added a matching gm_1BF9.c slice from 0x801BF948 to 0x801BFA6C, and added a follow-on gm_1BFA.c slice from 0x801BFA6C to 0x801BFFA8.
Updated configure.py to build Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gm_1BF9.c") and Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gm_1BFA.c") after gm_1A36.c.

Added src/melee/gm/gm_1BF9.c with matched implementations for gm_801BF948, gm_801BF9A8, and gm_801BFA3C plus MinorScene arrays gm_803DFDD8 and gm_803DFE18 and local static storage gm_804D6940/gm_804D6948.
Added src/melee/gm/gm_1BF9.h declaring gm_801BF948, gm_801BF9A8, and gm_801BFA3C with MinorScene* parameters.
Added src/melee/gm/gm_1BFA.c as a nonmatching placeholder file containing labels for gm_801BFA6C, gm_801BFABC, gm_801BFBA8, gm_801BFC60, gm_801BFCFC, and gm_801BFF7C.
Removed the gm_1BF9/gm_1BFA tail placeholders and the previous gm_801BF9A8 implementation from gm_1A36.c.
Changed gm_801A427C and gm_801A4284 from untyped raw pointer/M2C_FIELD accessors to typed MinorScene* helpers returning scene->unk_struct_0 and scene->unk_struct_1.
Moved MajorScene, MinorScene, MinorSceneHandler, SceneNums, GameState, and sceneData definitions out of gm_1A36.static.h into shared gm/types.h.
Added forward declarations for MajorScene, MinorScene, and MinorSceneHandler to gm/forward.h.
Added extern declaration for gmMainLib_8046B0F0 in gm/types.h so gm_1BF9.c can inspect gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x0.
smart_moves: Split the object at exact text/data/bss/sdata/sbss boundaries before marking only the proven gm_1BF9.c slice as Matching, leaving the remaining gm_1BFA.c range NonMatching.
Promoted scene structs from a static private header into gm/types.h once multiple translation units needed the same layouts.
Used MinorScene* in function signatures and accessors, replacing M2C_FIELD offset reads with named fields unk_struct_0 and unk_struct_1.
Kept unresolved later functions in a separate gm_1BFA.c placeholder instead of blocking the smaller matched gm_1BF9 range.
Preserved scene table data in the new matched file alongside the functions that reference it, making the split align with the configured data/sdata/sbss ranges.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large nonmatching game-mode file, update both splits.txt section ranges and configure.py object classification together; otherwise the new object layout will not reflect the intended match boundary.
If a matched slice needs structs previously hidden in a .static.h, move only the reusable type definitions into a shared types.h and keep file-local declarations/private helpers in the static header.
Typed scene-table access can improve both readability and matching confidence: gm_801A427C(scene) became scene->unk_struct_0 and gm_801A4284(scene) became scene->unk_struct_1.
A small matching island can be extracted while adjacent functions remain as nonmatching labels, provided the split boundaries cover the correct text and data ranges.
Scene callback prototypes should use MinorScene* when the callback receives a MinorScene entry rather than generic UNK_T/void*.
naming_conventions: New files follow address-range names: gm_1BF9.c/gm_1BF9.h for functions beginning at 0x801BF948 and gm_1BFA.c for the subsequent 0x801BFA6C range.
Function names remain address-based, such as gm_801BF948, gm_801BF9A8, and gm_801BFA3C.
Scene structs use project-level names MajorScene, MinorScene, MinorSceneHandler, SceneNums, GameState, and sceneData.
Unknown fields remain conservative with names like unk_struct_0, unk_struct_1, x0, x4, and x5 rather than overinterpreted semantic names.
Constants introduced in gm_1BF9.c use semantic enum names TROPHY_PIKMIN and LANG_JP.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Object split moved gm_1A36.c text end from 0x801BFFA8 to 0x801BF948 and created gm_1BF9.c text range 0x801BF948-0x801BFA6C.
Configured gm_1BF9.c as Matching while gm_1A36.c and gm_1BFA.c remain NonMatching.
Data range for gm_1BF9.c was isolated as 0x803DFDD8-0x803DFE48, matching the new MinorScene arrays gm_803DFDD8 and gm_803DFE18.
sdata/sbss ranges were also split so gm_1BF9.c owns 0x804D44C8-0x804D44E0 and 0x804D6940-0x804D6950, corresponding to the new static storage.
Replaced M2C_FIELD pointer arithmetic in gm_801A427C/gm_801A4284 with direct struct field access to support cleaner matching code.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no human text excerpt was provided, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match gm_1BFA.c functions gm_801BFA6C through gm_801BFF7C.
Find PRs that rename or further type unk_struct_0_t fields x0, x4, and x5 in gm_1BF9.c.
Find PRs that further refine MinorScene, MajorScene, MinorSceneHandler, or sceneData layouts in gm/types.h.
Find PRs that explain the scene exit callback gm_801A4330/lb_8001CE78 behavior.
Find PRs that replace address-based gm_801BF948/gm_801BF9A8/gm_801BFA3C names with semantic names.
